description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the docker:latest image with a docker:dind service and defining CONTAINER_TEST_IMAGE and CONTAINER_RELEASE_IMAGE variables. In the build stage add a build job that runs the global before_script `docker login -u gitlab-ci-token -p $CI_JOB_TOKEN registry.gitlab.com`, then executes `docker build --pull -t $CONTAINER_TEST_IMAGE .` and `docker push $CONTAINER_TEST_IMAGE`. Also in the build stage add a pages job that uses the python:alpine image, runs `pip install mkdocs mkdocs-rtd-dropdown`, then `mkdocs build` and `mv site public`, publishes the public/ directory as an artifact, and runs only on the master branch. In the test stage add a pylint job (python:3-slim, allow_failure) that runs `pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt`, `pip install --no-cache-dir pylint`, sets `FILES=$(find . -name "*.py")` and runs `pylint ${FILES}`; also add a unitests job that runs `docker pull $CONTAINER_TEST_IMAGE`, `docker network create pushfish`, `docker run -d --name mqtt --network pushfish toke/mosquitto:latest`, `docker run -e MQTT_ADDRESS='mqtt' --network pushfish $CONTAINER_TEST_IMAGE python tests.py`, `docker stop mqtt` and `docker rm mqtt`. Finally, in the release stage add a release-image job that runs only on master and executes `docker pull $CONTAINER_TEST_IMAGE`, `docker tag $CONTAINER_TEST_IMAGE $CONTAINER_RELEASE_IMAGE` and `docker push $CONTAINER_RELEASE_IMAGE`.
